summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements a comprehensive boundary audit system that detects project file mutations and blocks PRD readiness when evidence agents modify target files outside allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function captures SHA-256 digests of all project files except allowed_write_roots before agent execution
evidence.why: Provides pre-execution baseline for detecting mutations

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots function compares before/after file digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files
evidence.why: Identifies specific mutations that occurred during agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured before agent execution with output_dir excluded from mutation detection
evidence.why: Establishes clean baseline while allowing agent artifacts

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit compares snapshots and populates result.boundary with project_mutation status and changes list
evidence.why: Core boundary enforcement mechanism

evidence.summary: Boundary violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and force prd_ready=false even if agents returned passing results
evidence.why: Ensures boundary violations block PRD readiness

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses git ls-files for tracked project files; walkFiles provides filesystem fallback for non-git repositories
evidence.why: Supports both git-backed and non-git project environments

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary declares project_mutation=forbidden and restricts writes to output_dir only
evidence.why: Explicit boundary contract in dispatch plan

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates that all evidence records declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown
evidence.why: Prevents unscoped evidence from being accepted as fact

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult adds EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers when evidence records lack valid scope declaration
evidence.why: Blocks evidence without proper scope declarations

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines writes_code=false for all three evidence agent roles (explorer, cross-checker, verifier)
evidence.why: Protocol contract prohibits agents from writing project code

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns [explorer, cross-checker, verifier] for cross_check policy and [explorer, verifier] for single_agent
evidence.why: Cross-check mode requires three-agent validation

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness validates that required evidence agents completed successfully and checks evidence scope declarations
evidence.why: Final gatekeeper ensuring evidence quality before PRD readiness

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement detects conflicts between evidence agents (e.g., explorer says proceed, cross-checker says block)
evidence.why: Prevents averaging away of conflicting evidence

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass - writes unexpected-project-write.txt during agent execution
evidence.why: Proves boundary audit detects and blocks mutations even with passing agent results

evidence.summary: Test git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations - confirms git ls-files detects new files
evidence.why: Validates git-based boundary detection for untracked files

evidence.summary: Test non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts - excludes runtimeDir from mutation detection
evidence.why: Confirms non-git environments and artifact root exclusion work correctly

evidence.summary: Test agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization - removes scope field from evidence records
evidence.why: Verifies EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker generation

evidence.summary: Test agent conflict remains a readiness blocker after dispatch - explorer says proceed, cross-checker says block
evidence.why: Confirms evidence conflict detection prevents PRD readiness

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt includes explicit boundary rules: do not modify target project files, allowed artifact root specified, scope required for evidence
evidence.why: Agent instructions explicitly communicate boundary constraints

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile to boundary/research/full, sets claude_no_session_persistence=true, clears restrictive tool flags
evidence.why: Provider adapter configuration controls available tool sets

assumptions: The current implementation has not been stress-tested with real full-tool Claude agents in production
Boundary snapshot calculation performance may degrade on very large codebases (>100k files)
Evidence agents using full tool profile may have Write/Edit tool access despite boundary rules in prompts
risks: If boundary snapshot fails (git corruption, filesystem permission errors), mutations may go undetected
Evidence agents with full tool profile could use Write/Edit tools before boundary audit runs
Provider-level tool restrictions (--allowedTools) are cleared by default, relying only on prompt instructions for boundary enforcement
Evidence agents using research profile with external fetch/search could inject external evidence as project facts if validation fails
missing: Production evidence of real full-tool Claude agents being dispatched and blocked by boundary audit
Integration tests showing boundary behavior with actual Claude provider (not mocked spawnProviderPrompt)
Documentation on recovery actions when BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers are detected
Evidence of boundary audit performance characteristics on large monorepos

result.notes: Evidence dispatch boundary audit implementation is comprehensive and well-tested with mocked providers. Cross-check recommended because the claim involves security-sensitive boundary enforcement that has only been tested with mock providers, not real full-tool Claude agents.

agent_results.claim: The explorer's claim about comprehensive boundary audit implementation is confirmed, but a critical gap exists: the system relies only on prompt instructions to prevent full-tool agents from mutating project files, with no enforcement at the tool-restriction level.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot captures SHA-256 digests of all project files except output_dir before agent execution begins
evidence.why: Establishes pre-execution baseline for detecting mutations

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit compares snapshots and populates result.boundary with project_mutation status
evidence.why: Detects mutations that occurred during agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and force prd_ready=false regardless of agent results
evidence.why: Core enforcement mechanism that blocks PRD readiness on mutations

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile to boundary/research/full, sets claude_no_session_persistence=true, and clears claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools to empty strings
evidence.why: Confirms that tool restrictions are not set at the provider level; boundary enforcement relies only on prompt instructions

evidence.summary: Agent prompt includes explicit boundary rule but no technical enforcement: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files'
evidence.why: Shows boundary enforcement is contractual (prompt-based) not technical (tool-restriction based)

evidence.summary: executionConfig conditionally sets agent_tool_profile to 'full' when allowFullAgentTools=true and agentToolProfile includes 'full', 'all', 'write', or 'execution'
evidence.why: Full tool profile grants actual Write/Edit tool access while relying only on prompt instructions to prevent misuse

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation uses spawnProviderPrompt mock that directly writes unexpected-project-write.txt
evidence.why: Proves boundary detection works, but mutation is injected by test harness not by real agent tools

evidence.summary: All tests use spawnProviderPrompt mock option instead of real Claude provider invocation
evidence.why: Confirms no evidence of real full-tool Claude agent testing

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime uses options.spawnProviderPrompt || defaultSpawnProviderPrompt, defaulting to real provider invocation
evidence.why: Real providers can be invoked but no tests demonstrate this with full-tool agents

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs Claude command with optional --allowedTools flag but evidence-dispatch clears this to empty string
evidence.why: Technical tool restriction capability exists but is intentionally not used for evidence agents

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary declares project_mutation=forbidden and allowed_write_roots as output_dir only
evidence.why: Policy boundary exists but is enforced post-execution, not pre-execution at tool level

assumptions: Real full-tool Claude agents will respect prompt-based boundary rules despite having Write/Edit tool access
Post-execution boundary audit can detect all possible mutations before they cause damage
Agents cannot bypass boundary detection by modifying files then reverting them before audit completes
Claude provider will honor agent_tool_profile setting without additional tool restrictions
risks: Full-tool agents with Write/Edit access could mutate project files before boundary audit runs, relying only on prompt compliance
Agents running in full tool profile have technical capability to modify files despite boundary rules in prompts
Post-execution detection means damage occurs before violation is discovered
No evidence that real Claude agents in full tool mode will respect contractual boundary rules
Tool restrictions (claude_allowed_tools) are deliberately cleared, removing technical enforcement layer
missing: Integration tests with actual Claude provider (not spawnProviderPrompt mock) using full tool profile
Evidence of boundary enforcement holding against real full-tool Claude agents in production
Documentation on whether Claude provider respects agent_tool_profile without explicit --allowedTools
Test scenarios where full-tool agents attempt mutations and are blocked by something other than post-execution audit
Analysis of window between agent execution start and boundary snapshot completion for race conditions

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is confirmed and well-tested for detection, but a critical architectural gap exists: enforcement is post-execution (detect-and-block) not pre-execution (prevent). Full-tool agents have actual Write/Edit access and rely only on prompt instructions to avoid using them. This is a security-relevant finding because the claim suggests comprehensive protection when in fact the system has no technical prevention layer, only detection after the fact.

agent_results.claim: Both evidence agents properly separated facts from assumptions and documented all missing data. The cross-checker correctly identified a critical architectural gap that explorer did not emphasize: boundary enforcement is post-execution detection, not pre-execution prevention, and full-tool agents have actual Write/Edit tool access with only prompt-based restrictions.

evidence.summary: provider-adapter clears claude_allowed_tools to empty string, removing technical tool restrictions
evidence.why: Confirms cross-checker's finding that enforcement is prompt-based, not tool-restriction based

evidence.summary: executionConfig sets agent_tool_profile to 'full' when allowed, granting Write/Edit tool access
evidence.why: Verifies cross-checker's claim that agents have actual mutation capability

evidence.summary: Boundary audit runs post-execution, detecting mutations after they occur
evidence.why: Confirms cross-checker's finding that enforcement is detect-and-block, not prevent

evidence.summary: All tests use spawnProviderPrompt mock instead of real Claude provider
evidence.why: Validates both agents' finding that real full-tool agent behavior is untested

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime can use real provider via defaultSpawnProviderPrompt
evidence.why: Confirms real agent execution is possible but unverified

risks: If PRD proceeds assuming comprehensive pre-execution protection, production use could experience project mutations before detection triggers

Acceptance criterion 'Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked' is satisfied, but the criterion itself does not specify pre-execution vs post-execution enforcement
missing: Production evidence of boundary behavior with actual Claude provider and full-tool agents
Documentation clarifying that boundary enforcement is post-execution detection, not real-time prevention
Acceptance criteria specifying whether pre-execution prevention is required or post-execution detection is sufficient

result.notes: Evidence protocol completed successfully. Both agents properly cited project paths with line numbers, separated facts from assumptions, and documented missing data. Cross-checker correctly identified a critical architectural nuance: the system detects and blocks mutations post-execution rather than preventing them pre-execution. This is a factual finding about system design, not an evidence quality failure. PRD can proceed with cross-checker's 'blocked' verdict reflected in readiness blockers, as the acceptace criteria require boundary audit to block PRD readiness (which it does), not to prevent mutations pre-execution (which it does not). The missing data items are appropriate assumptions for PRD phase, not evidence protocol failures.

blockers.message: explorer reported unresolved missing evidence: Production evidence of real full-tool Claude agents being dispatched and blocked by boundary audit; Integration tests showing boundary behavior with actual Claude provider (not mocked spawnProviderPrompt); Documentation on recovery actions when BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers are detected; Evidence of boundary audit performance characteristics on large monorepos

cross-checker reported unresolved missing evidence: Integration tests with actual Claude provider (not spawnProviderPrompt mock) using full tool profile; Evidence of boundary enforcement holding against real full-tool Claude agents in production; Documentation on whether Claude provider respects agent_tool_profile without explicit --allowedTools; Test scenarios where full-tool agents attempt mutations and are blocked by something other than post-execution audit; Analysis of window between agent execution start and boundary snapshot completion for race conditions
cross-checker returned cross_check; keep PRD readiness blocked until resolved.

verifier reported unresolved missing evidence: Production evidence of boundary behavior with actual Claude provider and full-tool agents; Documentation clarifying that boundary enforcement is post-execution detection, not real-time prevention; Acceptance criteria specifying whether pre-execution prevention is required or post-execution detection is sufficient
